zh.lifeSummary: 當Lila Maxey於1580年出生在英國素福爾可科華士 希斯時，她的父親William Edgar Maxey15歲，母親Susan Ardella Robbins15歲。她於大約1598年與Benjamin Whitson在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1650年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 約克 可隆尼金斯布盧可林享年70歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lila Maxey于1580年出生在英国素福爾可科華士 希斯时，她的父亲William Edgar Maxey15岁，母亲Susan Ardella Robbins15岁。她于大约1598年与Benjamin Whitson在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1650年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 約克 可隆尼金斯布盧可林享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: Maxey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from a place in Northamptonshire, so named from the Old English personal name Maccus + Old English ēg ‘island’.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Macasa ‘descendant of Macus’, a personal name of uncertain origin. It may be a variant of Magnus .

variants.name: Magnus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Bernd, Egon, Erwin, Kurt, Otto, Reinhard, Volker, Wilhelm, Wolfgang.

German, Flemish, and Walloon: from the personal name Magnus, which is from the Latin adjective magnus ‘great’ or a Latinized form of a short form of ancient Germanic compound names based on the element magan ‘strength, might’. The name was borne by several early Christian saints, including St. Magnus (8th century), a monk in St. Gallen in Switzerland and missionary in Allgäu in southern Germany, whose real name was Magnoald. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). In Belgium, it may also be a Latinized (humanistic) form of Walloon Legrand .

English, Scottish, Swedish, Danish, Norwegian, and North German: from the Scandinavian personal name Magnus, borne by Magnus the Good (died 1047), king of Norway and Denmark, who was named for the Emperor Charlemagne, Latin Carolus Magnus ‘Charles the Great’, under the impression that Magnus was a personal name. The name spread from Norway to the eastern Scandinavian royal houses, and became popular all over Scandinavia and thence in the English Danelaw. It has also been adopted as a Jewish name.

variants.name: Maxie
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Maxey  .

nameMeanings.name: Lila
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Leila
meanings: Of Arabic origin, now fairly common in the English-speaking world, having been used as a name for an oriental beauty both by  Byron , in The Giaour (  1813 ) and Don Juan (  1819–24 ), and by  Lord Lytton  for the heroine of his novel Leila (  1838 ). In Arabic it means ‘night’, apparently alluding to a dark complexion.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Lila Maxey was born in 1580, in Forest Heath, Suffolk, England, her father, William Edgar Maxey, was 15 and her mother, Susan Ardella Robbins, was 15. She married Benjamin Whitson about 1598, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1650, in Brooklyn, Kings, New York Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 70.

ko.lifeSummary: Lila Maxey(이)가 1580년 영국 수폴크 포레스트 히스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Edgar Maxey(은)는 15세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Susan Ardella Robbins(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 대략 1598년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Benjamin Whitson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1650년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 요르크 콜론이 킨그스 브루클린에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lila Maxey родилась в 1580 г. в местности форест хит, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени William Edgar Maxey было 15 лет, а матери, которую звали Susan Ardella Robbins, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Benjamin Whitson около 1598 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1650 г. в местности бруклин, кингс, ню ерк колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 70 лет.
